title: Sinapis retrohispida
intro: Sinapis retrohispida, also known as the Himalayan mustard or Indian mustard, is a perennial plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its distinctive yellow flowers and pungent mustard oil. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Sinapis retrohispida include glucosinolates, isothiocyanates, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in the production of mustard oil, which is valued for its culinary and medicinal uses.
taxonomy: Sinapis retrohispida is classified in the family Brassicaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Sinapis, which includes several species of mustards. The family Brassicaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. The Brassicaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, including the Camelineae and the Brassiceae. Sinapis retrohispida is native to the Old World and is often found in dry, sandy areas.
